title: SA PDPL Art. 29 — cross-border transfer inventory and legal-basis evidence
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=SA PDPL, clause=Art. 29, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SA PDPL Art. 29 — UC provides continuous Splunk telemetry evidence for this regulatory requirement, uplifted from contributing to partial after review confirmed the SPL captures relevant monitoring signals.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SA PDPL Art. 29 (Cross-border personal data transfers) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.23: SA PDPL Art. 29 — cross-border transfer inventory and legal-basis evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.23' running on Data transfer ledger, cloud-region event logs, SDAIA notification records., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Data transfer ledger, cloud-region event logs, SDAIA notification records.
spl:
index=data_transfer earliest=-30d
| search src_jurisdiction="SA"
| eval basis_valid=if(legal_basis IN ("adequacy","scc","consent","exemption") AND isnotnull(legal_basis_ref),1,0)
| stats count AS transfers sum(basis_valid) AS with_basis BY dst_jurisdiction, data_class
| eval compliance_pct=round(100*with_basis/transfers,1)
| where compliance_pct < 100
description: Inventory-style view of cross-border transfers from SA with the lawful basis attached; flags any transfer without an Art. 29 basis.
value: Art. 29 requires a documented basis for every outbound transfer; continuous inventory is superior to point-in-time attestations.
implementation: (1) Emit every outbound transfer event with src_jurisdiction, dst_jurisdiction, legal_basis, legal_basis_ref; (2) schedule daily. Indexes required: `index=data_transfer`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `basis_valid`, `compliance_pct`, `data_class`, `dst_jurisdiction`, `transfers`, `with_basis`.
knownFalsePositives: Tier-2 framework drift during quarterly reviews, scheduled crosswalk updates, or controlled exception cycles.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Data transfer ledger, cloud-region event logs, SDAIA notification records..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Emit every outbound transfer event with src_jurisdiction, dst_jurisdiction, legal_basis, legal_basis_ref; (2) schedule daily.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=data_transfer earliest=-30d
| search src_jurisdiction="SA"
| eval basis_valid=if(legal_basis IN ("adequacy","scc","consent","exemption") AND isnotnull(legal_basis_ref),1,0)
| stats count AS transfers sum(basis_valid) AS with_basis BY dst_jurisdiction, data_class
| eval compliance_pct=round(100*with_basis/transfers,1)
| where compliance_pct < 100
```

Understanding this SPL

**SA PDPL Art. 29 — cross-border transfer inventory and legal-basis evidence** — Art. 29 requires a documented basis for every outbound transfer; continuous inventory is superior to point-in-time attestations.

Documented **Data sources**: Data transfer ledger, cloud-region event logs, SDAIA notification records. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: data_transfer.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=data_transfer, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **basis_valid** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by dst_jurisdiction, data_class** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **compliance_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where compliance_pct < 100` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
grandmaExplanation: We help you show how a second-line framework line maps to what you actually monitor, so your gap story stays clear.
